description: You are a ground data systems engineer evaluating the compressibility of a live spacecraft telemetry stream before deploying a 'Delta Compression' algorithm update. Collect 25 sequential time-series samples of the 'INST HEALTH_STATUS TEMP1' telemetry item in OpenC3 COSMOS, waiting 0.5 to 1.0 seconds between readings. Analyze the data by computing 24 sequential deltas (delta[i] = sample[i+1] - sample[i]) and summary statistics: 'max_abs_delta' (maximum absolute value), 'mean_abs_delta' (average absolute value), and 'zero_delta_count' (count of deltas equal to 0.0). Set 'compression_recommended' to true if 'mean_abs_delta' < 0.5. Save the results to /home/ga/Desktop/compression_analysis.json with the following keys: 'target' ('INST'), 'packet' ('HEALTH_STATUS'), 'item' ('TEMP1'), 'sample_count' (25), 'raw_samples', 'deltas', 'statistics' (containing the three computed metrics), and 'compression_recommended'. Ensure all calculations use the exact values recorded in the 'raw_samples' array to prevent mathematical mismatch errors. OpenC3 password: Cosmos2024!
